description: Declarative, versioned risk-classification policy for GraphSmith repair auto-apply eligibility (plan §3.3). This is a SYNTACTIC ALLOWLIST, NOT a proof of safety: it can only demonstrate the ABSENCE of statically-detectable external-call and obfuscation constructs in a payload. Its role is to gate which repairs may be auto-applied without a human; every gate is fail-closed (any doubt => not eligible). Canonical source aligned with scripts/heal.js STATIC_UNPROVABLE_PATTERNS / EXTERNAL_CALL_PATTERNS and scripts/graphlint.js R5 (eval/exec/new-require ban) in spirit; heal.js and scaffold.js keep their own inline copies today and may converge onto this file in a later pass.

typed.description: Structured, non-executable repair surfaces: prompts, tunables, scenario data, config, and other declarative data files. Typed repairs are the ONLY repair_class that can ever be auto-apply-eligible, and only when capabilityScan also proves no_external_calls.

code.description: Any executable surface, including the manager control-plane and any file GraphSmith cannot positively classify as typed (fail-closed default). code repairs are NEVER auto-apply-eligible in v0.2.0, regardless of capabilityScan result.

manager_detection.description: Manager/control-plane paths are detected case-insensitively and path-normalized so MANAGER.js, Manager.js, nested/manager.JS, manager.cjs, manager.mjs etc. never fall through to 'regular code'. Manager targets are always repair_class=code, kind=manager, is_manager=true and are hard-refused for auto-apply upstream of this policy.

classification_rules.note: Fail-closed default: anything not positively classified typed is treated as code so it is never auto-applied silently.

auto_apply_eligibility.rule: A repair is auto-apply-eligible ONLY when repair_class == 'typed' AND capabilityScan(payload).no_external_calls == true (no external-call pattern match AND no unprovable-construct match). Any repair_class == 'code' (including is_manager == true) is NEVER auto-apply-eligible in v0.2.0, regardless of scan result -- code always requires a human gate.

external_call_patterns.description: Syntactic signals of an external effect (filesystem, network, process, dynamic module load, eval). ANY match => not statically provable free of external-call involvement. Sourced from scripts/heal.js EXTERNAL_CALL_PATTERNS.

unprovable_constructs.description: Constructs that DEFEAT static proof of external-call absence (obfuscation / indirection). ANY match => fail-closed NOT eligible, even with zero external_call_patterns matches. Sourced from scripts/heal.js STATIC_UNPROVABLE_PATTERNS.

bounds.description: Upper bound on capabilityScan input size (UTF-8 byte length of the joined text). Input at or above this bound is treated fail-closed (reported as the synthetic unprovable construct 'input-too-large') rather than regex-scanned, to bound worst-case scan cost deterministically.

Cap on the number of distinct matched-pattern ids returned per array (external-call matches, unprovable-construct matches) in capabilityScan output. Matching still fails closed beyond the cap; this bounds output size only, in stable declared-pattern order.
